authoritative_sources.title: Getting Started Checklist
authoritative_sources.publisher: Child Care Technical Assistance Network / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Office of Child Care technical-assistance guidance naming Child Care Automated Reporting System (CARS) and related reporting systems used by CCDF administrators.

authoritative_sources.title: What Is Child Care Licensing?
authoritative_sources.publisher: ChildCare.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official ACF guidance defining licensed and license-exempt care, minimum health and safety expectations, and the monitoring baseline used to interpret provider comparability and capacity metrics.

authoritative_sources.title: Monitoring and Inspections
authoritative_sources.publisher: ChildCare.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official ACF guidance identifying inspection reports, complaints, violations, and corrective actions as named child-care records that materially change analytics posture.
used_for: escalation when compliance posture changes the answer

authoritative_sources.title: Program Information Report (PIR)
authoritative_sources.publisher: HeadStart.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Head Start reporting guidance for funded enrollment, attendance, demographics, and service fields used in Head Start and mixed-model child-care analytics.

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR Part 1303
authoritative_sources.publisher: HeadStart.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Head Start confidentiality and records regulation providing authoritative child-record privacy and disclosure boundaries for analytics outputs touching Head Start data.

authoritative_sources.title: Planning your Market Rate Survey
authoritative_sources.publisher: Child Care Technical Assistance Network / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Office of Child Care guidance directly tied to CCDF market-rate survey workflow, including the source classes and collection choices that shape affordability and rate-comparison analytics.
used_for: scenario design for unsupported rate claims

authoritative_sources.title: Increasing Subsidy Payments and Setting Rates
authoritative_sources.publisher: Child Care Technical Assistance Network / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official OCC-backed guidance linking payment rates to market-rate surveys or alternative methodologies, which constrains how subsidy-rate benchmarks and affordability narratives can be stated.

authoritative_sources.title: National Database of Child Care Licensing Regulations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ACF's licensing regulations database is the public cross-jurisdiction source for locating state and territory licensing rules that change provider-capacity, staffing, and compliance-sensitive metrics.
used_for: provider-type and age-band rule lookup assumptions

authoritative_sources.title: Caring for Our Children
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Resource Center for Health and Safety in Child Care and Early Education
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized national child-care health and safety standard set used to tighten terminology and evidence framing after official ACF and Head Start sources.

workflow_stages.name: Source collection and validation
workflow_stages.description: Collect enrollment, attendance, subsidy, Head Start, licensing, staffing, quality, and incident records and determine whether the evidence set is current and sufficient for analytics.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Metric definition and source hierarchy
workflow_stages.description: Resolve metric logic, denominator rules, funding-model differences, date windows, and source precedence before analytical or narrative claims are assembled.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Analytical package assembly
workflow_stages.description: Build trend notes, variance summaries, utilization packages, and exception packages while preserving provider type, licensing, ratio, funding, and attendance context.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Narrative synthesis and disclosure-risk review
workflow_stages.description: Check that no unsupported subsidy, quality, benchmark, causal, safety, or disclosure language appears and that privacy controls and confidence posture match the evidence set.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception and adjacent-authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate stale, conflicting, incomplete, privacy-sensitive, licensing-sensitive, safety-sensitive, or subsidy-sensitive cases instead of synthesizing unsupported certainty.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: child, family, provider, classroom, and program identifier records
enrollment rosters, funded-slot records, waitlists, vacancy trackers, and attendance logs
CCDF subsidy authorizations, provider-payment records, co-payment context, and attendance-claim extracts
Head Start PIR inputs, funded enrollment records, attendance and chronic absence records, and vacant-slot notes
licensing status records, inspection reports, complaint findings, corrective-action records, and licensing rule lookups
staffing rosters, ratio worksheets, training and background-check status, and classroom coverage records
QRIS or accreditation status records, family survey or family-service touchpoint records, and quality-improvement evidence
incident logs, safety review notes, disclosure approvals, and analytical caveat or escalation notes
systems_of_record: child care management or attendance platform
CCDF subsidy and payment reporting system
Head Start reporting system and PIR workflow
state or territory licensing portal or public provider database
document control and report publication repository

allowed: Classify child-care analytics requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Define or validate metrics, source hierarchy, denominator logic, refresh assumptions, and caveat language from child-care records.
Prepare analytical briefs, reconciliation notes, and disclosure-risk escalations without overstating subsidy, licensing, safety, or privacy authority.
escalate: Required tenant facts, record locations, metric definitions, benchmark methodology, or source precedence rules are missing and materially change the output.
Licensing status, inspection posture, serious incidents, subsidy authorization posture, or disclosure limits materially change the analytical answer.
Public guidance, jurisdiction rules, or tenant policy conflict on metric eligibility, provider status, publication posture, or disclosure language.
Requests cross into subsidy eligibility, licensing corrective action, legal interpretation, safety authority, or final program approval.
refuse: Requests to imply subsidy award, provider-operating approval, licensing compliance, safety clearance, or final QRIS or accreditation status.
Requests to publish unsupported causal, benchmark, policy, access, or quality claims not supported by the evidence set.
Requests to disclose child-identifying or small-cell records without explicit disclosure authority and aggregation rules.

domain_failure_modes: Publishing utilization or access metrics even though denominator logic is disputed across funded enrollment, licensed capacity, staffed capacity, and authorized care.
Treating stale subsidy, attendance, licensing, or incident records as current and overstating provider performance or child-care access.
Comparing providers with different licensing posture, funding model, or provider type as if they shared one uniform operating baseline.
Turning analytics into subsidy, licensing, safety, or legal authority instead of staying inside evidence-backed analysis.
Ignoring privacy and small-cell disclosure risk when child-level or center-level breakdowns are requested.
Producing generic dashboard language that drops provider, classroom, funding, licensing, attendance, and ratio semantics specific to child care.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting
Stakeholder asks for a claim or disclosure not supported by the data
Different systems disagree on the metric outcome
A stakeholder asks for stronger conclusions than the data supports

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific licensing and QRIS definitions vary by state, tribe, territory, and provider type.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Provider comparison, quality posture, ratio assumptions, and capacity logic can change materially.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Treat as tenant-supplied retrieval input and escalate rather than guessing when jurisdiction context is missing.

notes: Research constrains the lane to child-care-specific records and prevents generic analytics behavior that ignores provider type, funding mix, or licensing posture.
No blocking unresolved ambiguities remain for a validated spec pack because jurisdiction-specific variation is handled as a required runtime input and escalation condition.
